title: Save & Load from File
description: Add file persistence to the contact manager. Create save_contacts() that writes contacts to a text file (one contact per line, fields separated by '|'), and load_contacts() that reads them back.
starter_code:
# Contact Manager — Step 6: Save & Load from File
# Use file I/O to persist contacts between sessions.

import os

contacts = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'phone': '555-0003', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'},
]

def save_contacts(contact_list, filename):
    """Save contacts to a text file.
    Each line: name|phone|email
    """
    # Your code here — open file for writing
    pass

def load_contacts(filename):
    """Load contacts from a text file.
    Returns a list of contact dicts.
    Returns empty list if file doesn't exist.
    """
    # Your code here — open file for reading
    return []

# Test it
save_contacts(contacts, 'test_contacts.txt')
loaded = load_contacts('test_contacts.txt')
print(f"Saved {len(contacts)}, loaded {len(loaded)}")
for c in loaded:
    print(f"  {c['name']} - {c['phone']} - {c['email']}")

# Clean up test file
os.remove('test_contacts.txt')
test_code:
import os
test_file = '_test_proj_contacts.txt'
try:
    test_data = [
        {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
        {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
    ]
    save_contacts(test_data, test_file)
    assert os.path.exists(test_file), 'save_contacts should create the file'
    loaded = load_contacts(test_file)
    assert isinstance(loaded, list), 'load_contacts should return a list'
    assert len(loaded) == 2, f'Should load 2 contacts, got {len(loaded)}'
    assert loaded[0]['name'] == 'Alice', f"First contact name should be Alice, got {loaded[0]['name']}"
    assert loaded[0]['phone'] == '555-0001', 'First contact phone should match'
    assert loaded[0]['email'] == 'alice@example.com', 'First contact email should match'
    assert loaded[1]['name'] == 'Bob', 'Second contact should be Bob'
    empty = load_contacts('_nonexistent_file.txt')
    assert empty == [], 'load_contacts should return [] for missing file'
finally:
    if os.path.exists(test_file):
        os.remove(test_file)
acceptance_criteria: save_contacts() writes contacts to a text file (one per line, pipe-separated)
load_contacts() reads contacts back into a list of dicts
load_contacts() returns empty list if the file doesn't exist
Data survives a round-trip: save then load produces identical data
hints: Use open(filename, 'w') to write — use a with statement to auto-close
For loading: use open(filename, 'r') and line.strip().split('|')
Wrap load in try/except FileNotFoundError to handle missing files
